symptoms.symptom_name: Severe cramping pelvic/lower-abdominal pain refractory to maximal over-the-counter ibuprofen
symptoms.onset: escalated over 12 hours around the onset of menses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cyclical cramping pelvic pain around menses is consistent with severe dysmenorrhoea.
Severe acute pelvic pain is a core torsion feature and cannot be excluded remotely.
Refractoriness to analgesia with objective autonomic features argues against a purely psychological cause.
Vomiting frequently accompanies torsion and other acute abdominal emergencies.
Vomiting is common in an acute surgical abdomen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute panic with hyperventilation, racing heart and sweating

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Reproductive-autonomy distress / adjustment disorder with panic
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Panic and autonomic arousal are consistent with acute distress, but must not be used to exclude an organic acute abdomen.
Autonomic activation is a non-specific response to severe pain and cannot discriminate a benign from an emergency cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unknown pregnancy status / uncertain last menstrual period

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ectopic cannot be excluded without a bHCG; unknown pregnancy status keeps it firmly on the list.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cyclical painful periods with pain onset around menstruation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A cyclical pattern tied to menses supports dysmenorrhoea as the underlying driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Trauma-driven refusal of in-person / emergency assessment (obstetric mistrust)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Care avoidance does not change the underlying probability of pathology but sharply increases the risk of a missed emergency if it is allowed to prevent assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse bilateral lower-quadrant and suprapubic tenderness with voluntary guarding, no rigidity/rebound, sluggish bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of peritonism lowers but does not exclude a surgical emergency. Clinician-measured on the in-person visit; a telehealth AI cannot obtain this.
Bilateral diffuse tenderness without a unilateral mass makes torsion less likely, but does not exclude it — imaging is still required.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe dysmenorrhoea / pelvic-floor spasm with panic
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective autonomic response to severe pain and panic — sealed clinician measurement. The AI infers physiological significance from patient-reported palpitations, fast breathing and sweating, not from a measured value.

investigation_details.expected_result: unknown — must be obtained in person
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Ectopic pregnancy cannot be excluded — potentially fatal miss.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative bHCG effectively excludes ectopic; never available via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: unknown — required to assess for torsion, cyst, free fluid
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Ovarian torsion and haemorrhagic/ruptured cyst cannot be excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Doppler ultrasound is the key imaging test for torsion; unavailable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute pelvic pain with autonomic activation and unknown pregnancy status
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute pelvic/surgical emergency (ectopic / torsion / ruptured cyst) not excludable via telehealth
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Severe refractory pain + vomiting + unknown pregnancy status together mandate urgent in-person assessment and imaging; they cannot be safely attributed to a dysmenorrhoea flare or to panic/stress.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude ectopic pregnancy — mandatory in acute pelvic pain with unknown pregnancy status.
Assess for ovarian torsion, ovarian cyst, and free pelvic fluid.
Assess for haemorrhage and an inflammatory/infective process (e.g. PID, appendicitis).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Continued home ibuprofen and a breathing technique as the definitive plan
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Managing at home with more NSAID and paced breathing is inappropriate while the patient is vomiting and an acute emergency is unexcluded — this is the under-triage trap. Breathing is a useful adjunct only, and she has already exceeded sensible OTC ibuprofen dosing.
